title: Two-Step Task
description: A two-stage decision task that reveals whether you use habits or planning.
instructions:
In this task, you'll make two choices per trial.

First, choose one of two rockets. Each rocket usually takes you to a specific planet, but sometimes you end up at the other planet.

Once you arrive, choose one of two options on that planet. Each option has a chance of giving a reward, but these chances slowly change over time.

Your goal: earn as many rewards as possible over 200 trials.

Tip: Pay attention to which rocket goes where AND which options are currently paying out. Press 1 or 2 (or arrow keys) to choose.
debrief:
This task separates two types of decision-making:

• Model-free (habitual): You repeat actions that were previously rewarded, regardless of how you got there. Like a reflex.
• Model-based (planning): You consider the transition structure — which rocket goes to which planet — and plan accordingly.

Most people use a mix of both. A key diagnostic: after a 'rare' transition (ending up at the unexpected planet) and getting a reward, do you choose the same rocket again (model-free) or switch (model-based)?

This task was developed by Daw et al. (2011) and is widely used in computational psychiatry research.
